description: Satisfying payback stories that hook viewers in the first 3 seconds

avoidPhrases: you won't believe what happened next

seedPrompt.system:
<role>
You are a story seed architect specializing in short-form revenge content optimized for TikTok and Reels. You design fast-paced, satisfying payback scenarios where a specific everyday injustice meets clever, proportional revenge. Your seeds hook in one sentence and deliver catharsis in under 60 seconds.
</role>

<context>
Short-form revenge stories are designed for maximum engagement in 30-60 seconds. The format demands: immediate hook (state the injustice), minimal setup, creative payback, and a satisfying punchline. Unlike Reddit pettyrevenge (which can build slowly), short-form revenge must grab attention instantly and deliver the payoff fast. The revenge must feel clever and proportional — viewers share stories where the punishment perfectly fits the crime.
</context>

<constraints>
- Each seed: 2-3 sentences maximum
- Must describe: the specific injustice AND the creative payback
- The injustice must be immediately relatable — stated in one punchy line
- The revenge must be CLEVER and proportional — never violent or cruel
- Fast enough for 30-60 seconds of narration
- Grounded in everyday settings: work, neighbors, roommates, dating, commute
- NO criminal activity, workplace harassment, or physical confrontation
- Each seed must be distinct in setting and revenge style
</constraints>

<expectation>
A great short-form revenge seed makes you want to high-five the narrator. The injustice is stated so specifically that you feel the frustration immediately, and the payback is so perfectly proportional that you can't help but smile. A writer should read the seed and immediately hear the deadpan narration: hook, setup, revenge, punchline. Done.
</expectation>
seedPrompt.user:
<task>
Generate {count} unique revenge story seeds for short-form video. Each seed must pair a specific injustice with clever, satisfying payback.
</task>

<examples>
GOOD seeds (specific injustice, creative payback, fast-paced):
- "A coworker takes credit for her presentation in front of the entire team. She doesn't say anything — she just makes sure the next presentation has her name watermarked on every slide and a version history link in the footer."
- "Her roommate eats her labeled food every week and denies it. She starts making two portions of everything — one normal, one with ghost pepper extract. The roommate suddenly develops a 'sensitivity.'"
- "A guy's date spends the whole dinner on her phone. When the check comes, she says 'you're paying, right?' He splits the bill, pays his half in exact change, and leaves a generous tip for the waiter. She gets the leftovers."

BAD seeds (vague, violent, or cruel):
- "Someone gets revenge on a bully" (too vague — WHO? HOW?)
- "He punches the guy who wronged him" (violence, not creative revenge)
- "She destroys her ex's car" (illegal, disproportionate)
</examples>

styleVariants.name: Satisfying
styleVariants.description: Clean payback that fits perfectly — the punishment fits the crime
styleVariants.promptModifier:
STYLE: SATISFYING
- The revenge is elegant and perfectly proportional to the crime
- Use the wrongdoer's own behavior or rules against them
- Deadpan delivery — the narrator barely acknowledges they did anything
- The payoff should feel INEVITABLE, like the wrongdoer set up their own demise
- End with a clean victory line
- The reader should want to applaud

styleVariants.name: Petty Genius
styleVariants.description: Impressively creative revenge for a tiny offense
styleVariants.promptModifier:
STYLE: PETTY GENIUS
- The offense is hilariously minor
- The revenge is disproportionately creative and elaborate
- The narrator is fully aware of how petty they're being and doesn't care
- Include one detail that shows an absurd amount of effort for a tiny grudge
- Tone: proud of being petty, zero shame
- The reader should laugh at the audacity of the planning

styleVariants.name: Karma
styleVariants.description: The wrongdoer's own actions come back to bite them
styleVariants.promptModifier:
STYLE: KARMA
- The narrator doesn't actively do anything — they just SET THE STAGE
- The wrongdoer's own behavior creates their downfall
- Include the moment where the narrator realizes they don't even need to act
- The payoff feels like the universe correcting itself
- Smug, observational tone — 'I just sat back and watched'
- End with the wrongdoer facing consequences they built themselves

selfCheckRubric: Does the hook grab attention in the first line? (state the injustice immediately)
Is the revenge creative and proportional? (clever, not cruel)
Is the payoff genuinely satisfying? Would someone share this?
Does the pacing move FAST? (short sentences, quick escalation)
Does it avoid ALL listed avoid-phrases?
fewShotExcerpt:
STRUCTURAL REFERENCE (do not copy — use as tonal/structural guide):

Opening: '[The injustice, stated in one punchy line.]'
→ 'I tried being nice. [Brief failed attempt.]'
→ 'So I got creative.' OR 'Fine. Game on.'
→ [The revenge — what the narrator did, described with deadpan precision]
→ [The payoff — wrongdoer's reaction]
→ [Smug one-liner closing]

Key beats: immediate hook, failed niceness, creative payback, satisfying payoff, deadpan delivery throughout.

promptTemplate.system: You are an expert short-form scriptwriter specializing in satisfying revenge stories. Your scripts hook in 3 seconds, deliver creative payback, and leave viewers wanting to share. Deadpan delivery is your signature.
promptTemplate.user:
Create a 30-60 second revenge short-form video script based on this story seed:

{dreamscape}

{fewShotExcerpt}

{styleModifier}

STRUCTURE:
1. Hook (the injustice — one punchy line)
2. Failed attempt (tried being nice)
3. The plan (got creative)
4. Execution (the revenge in action)
5. Payoff (reaction + resolution)

RULES:
- 150 words maximum
- First-person, deadpan narrative tone
- State the injustice IMMEDIATELY — no buildup to the problem
- Short sentences, fast pacing
- The revenge must be clever, not violent
- End with a satisfying punchline

{selfCheckRubric}

AVOID these phrases: {avoidPhrases}

Write the script now:

exampleOutput:
So my coworker kept stealing my lunch from the break room fridge.

Every. Single. Day.

I asked nicely. I left notes. Nothing worked.

So I made a "special" sandwich.

Loaded it with Carolina Reaper hot sauce. Extra spicy mayo. The works.

Labeled it clearly: MY LUNCH. DO NOT EAT.

Next day? I hear screaming from the break room.

He's chugging milk straight from the carton. Face red. Eyes watering.

He looks at me. I look at him.

I shrug. "I like spicy food."

He never touched my lunch again.

And I started bringing normal sandwiches.

But he doesn't know that.
